title: What is the box model?
options: Every element is a rectangular box consisting of content, padding, border, and margin from inside out
correct_answer: Every element is a rectangular box consisting of content, padding, border, and margin from inside out

title: What is box-sizing: border-box?
options: Makes width/height include padding and border; total size stays as specified
Changes the box model to content-box
correct_answer: Makes width/height include padding and border; total size stays as specified

title: What is the difference between inline, block, and inline-block?
description: How do these display values differ?
options: Block takes full width + new line; inline flows with text, no width/height; inline-block flows inline but accepts width/height
correct_answer: Block takes full width + new line; inline flows with text, no width/height; inline-block flows inline but accepts width/height

title: What is the CSS specificity hierarchy?
description: Order of CSS specificity from highest to lowest.
correct_answer: !important > inline styles > #id > .class/:pseudo-class/[attr] > element/::pseudo-element > *

title: What is Flexbox?
options: A one-dimensional layout system that distributes space between items along a main axis (row or column)
correct_answer: A one-dimensional layout system that distributes space between items along a main axis (row or column)

title: What does flex: 1 mean?
options: Shorthand for flex-grow: 1, flex-shrink: 1, flex-basis: 0%; the item will grow to fill available space equally with other flex: 1 items
correct_answer: Shorthand for flex-grow: 1, flex-shrink: 1, flex-basis: 0%; the item will grow to fill available space equally with other flex: 1 items

title: What is the difference between justify-content and align-items?
description: How do they differ in flexbox?
options: justify-content aligns items along the main axis; align-items aligns items along the cross axis
correct_answer: justify-content aligns items along the main axis; align-items aligns items along the cross axis

title: What is CSS Grid?
options: A two-dimensional layout system that handles both rows and columns simultaneously
correct_answer: A two-dimensional layout system that handles both rows and columns simultaneously

title: What is grid-template-areas?
options: Defines named grid areas using a string-based visual layout syntax, making complex layouts readable
correct_answer: Defines named grid areas using a string-based visual layout syntax, making complex layouts readable

title: What is the fr unit in CSS Grid?
options: Fraction: represents a fraction of available space in the grid container
correct_answer: Fraction: represents a fraction of available space in the grid container

title: What is the difference between em and rem?
description: How do em and rem differ?
options: em is relative to the parent element's font size; rem is relative to the root element's font size
correct_answer: em is relative to the parent element's font size; rem is relative to the root element's font size

title: What are CSS custom properties (variables)?
options: Declared with --name and accessed with var(--name); they cascade and can be scoped to any selector
correct_answer: Declared with --name and accessed with var(--name); they cascade and can be scoped to any selector

title: What is the position property?
description: Explain the values of the CSS position property.
options: static (default, normal flow), relative (offset from normal), absolute (relative to nearest positioned ancestor), fixed (relative to viewport), sticky (switches between relative and fixed)
correct_answer: static (default, normal flow), relative (offset from normal), absolute (relative to nearest positioned ancestor), fixed (relative to viewport), sticky (switches between relative and fixed)

title: What is the z-index?
options: Controls the stacking order of positioned elements; higher values appear in front; only works on positioned (non-static) elements
correct_answer: Controls the stacking order of positioned elements; higher values appear in front; only works on positioned (non-static) elements

title: What is a stacking context?
options: A three-dimensional conceptualization of HTML elements along the z-axis; created by positioned elements with z-index, opacity < 1, transform, filter, etc.
All elements are in one context
correct_answer: A three-dimensional conceptualization of HTML elements along the z-axis; created by positioned elements with z-index, opacity < 1, transform, filter, etc.

title: What is the ::before pseudo-element?
options: Creates a pseudo-element that is the first child of the selected element; requires the content property
Adds an element before the document
correct_answer: Creates a pseudo-element that is the first child of the selected element; requires the content property

title: What are CSS transitions?
options: Smoothly animate property changes over a specified duration using transition-property, transition-duration, transition-timing-function, and transition-delay
correct_answer: Smoothly animate property changes over a specified duration using transition-property, transition-duration, transition-timing-function, and transition-delay

title: What are CSS animations?
options: Define animation states using @keyframes with from/to or percentage steps, applied via the animation shorthand property
correct_answer: Define animation states using @keyframes with from/to or percentage steps, applied via the animation shorthand property

title: What is the will-change property?
options: Hints to the browser that an element's property will change, allowing it to optimize rendering (e.g., promote to its own compositor layer)
correct_answer: Hints to the browser that an element's property will change, allowing it to optimize rendering (e.g., promote to its own compositor layer)

title: What is the clamp() function?
description: What does clamp(min, preferred, max) do?
options: Clamps a value between a minimum and maximum: returns min if preferred < min, max if preferred > max, otherwise preferred
correct_answer: Clamps a value between a minimum and maximum: returns min if preferred < min, max if preferred > max, otherwise preferred

title: What is the :has() selector?
options: A parent selector that matches elements containing children matching the argument; e.g., div:has(> img) selects divs with direct img children
correct_answer: A parent selector that matches elements containing children matching the argument; e.g., div:has(> img) selects divs with direct img children

title: What is the :is() selector?
options: A functional pseudo-class that takes a selector list and matches any element matched by one of the selectors, reducing repetition
correct_answer: A functional pseudo-class that takes a selector list and matches any element matched by one of the selectors, reducing repetition

title: What is the :where() selector?
options: :where() is identical to :is() except its specificity is always zero, making it easy to override
correct_answer: :where() is identical to :is() except its specificity is always zero, making it easy to override

title: What is container queries?
description: What do CSS container queries (@container) enable?
options: Style elements based on the size of their container rather than the viewport, enabling truly component-based responsive design
correct_answer: Style elements based on the size of their container rather than the viewport, enabling truly component-based responsive design

title: What are CSS layers (@layer)?
options: Defines cascade layers to control the order in which CSS rules take effect, regardless of specificity or source order
correct_answer: Defines cascade layers to control the order in which CSS rules take effect, regardless of specificity or source order

title: What is the aspect-ratio property?
options: Sets a preferred aspect ratio for an element, automatically calculating height from width (or vice versa)
correct_answer: Sets a preferred aspect ratio for an element, automatically calculating height from width (or vice versa)

title: What is the gap property?
description: What does gap do in flexbox and grid?
options: Sets the spacing between flex/grid items without adding margin to the outer edges; shorthand for row-gap and column-gap
Creates empty space outside the container
correct_answer: Sets the spacing between flex/grid items without adding margin to the outer edges; shorthand for row-gap and column-gap

title: What is the object-fit property?
options: Specifies how replaced elements like img/video should be resized to fit their container: fill, contain, cover, none, scale-down
correct_answer: Specifies how replaced elements like img/video should be resized to fit their container: fill, contain, cover, none, scale-down

title: What is the difference between visibility: hidden and display: none?
options: display: none removes the element from the document flow; visibility: hidden hides it but preserves its space
correct_answer: display: none removes the element from the document flow; visibility: hidden hides it but preserves its space

title: What is the difference between opacity: 0 and visibility: hidden?
options: opacity: 0 makes the element transparent but it still receives pointer events; visibility: hidden makes it invisible and ignores pointer events
correct_answer: opacity: 0 makes the element transparent but it still receives pointer events; visibility: hidden makes it invisible and ignores pointer events

title: What is a media query?
options: Apply CSS rules conditionally based on device characteristics like viewport width, orientation, resolution, or color scheme
correct_answer: Apply CSS rules conditionally based on device characteristics like viewport width, orientation, resolution, or color scheme

title: What is the prefers-color-scheme media query?
options: Detects whether the user's OS is set to light or dark mode, enabling automatic theme switching
correct_answer: Detects whether the user's OS is set to light or dark mode, enabling automatic theme switching

title: What is the prefers-reduced-motion media query?
options: Detects if the user has requested minimal animation in their OS settings, for accessibility
correct_answer: Detects if the user has requested minimal animation in their OS settings, for accessibility

title: What is the semantic HTML?
options: HTML elements that clearly describe their meaning (header, nav, main, article, section, footer) improve accessibility, SEO, and code readability
correct_answer: HTML elements that clearly describe their meaning (header, nav, main, article, section, footer) improve accessibility, SEO, and code readability

title: What is the <picture> element?
description: What does the picture element do?
options: Provides multiple image sources for responsive images, allowing the browser to choose the best source based on viewport, resolution, or format support
correct_answer: Provides multiple image sources for responsive images, allowing the browser to choose the best source based on viewport, resolution, or format support

title: What is the srcset attribute?
description: What does srcset do on an img tag?
options: Provides a set of image sources with descriptors (width or pixel density) so the browser can select the most appropriate image
correct_answer: Provides a set of image sources with descriptors (width or pixel density) so the browser can select the most appropriate image

title: What is the loading='lazy' attribute?
options: Defers loading of off-screen images/iframes until the user scrolls near them, improving initial page load performance
correct_answer: Defers loading of off-screen images/iframes until the user scrolls near them, improving initial page load performance

title: What is the <dialog> element?
description: What does the dialog element provide?
options: A native modal/non-modal dialog box with built-in show(), showModal(), and close() methods, backdrop, and focus trapping
correct_answer: A native modal/non-modal dialog box with built-in show(), showModal(), and close() methods, backdrop, and focus trapping

title: What is the <details> and <summary> elements?
options: A native disclosure widget (accordion): clicking the summary toggles visibility of the details content, no JavaScript needed
correct_answer: A native disclosure widget (accordion): clicking the summary toggles visibility of the details content, no JavaScript needed

title: What are ARIA attributes?
description: What does ARIA stand for and why is it used?
options: Accessible Rich Internet Applications: HTML attributes that provide extra semantics for assistive technologies when native HTML isn't sufficient
correct_answer: Accessible Rich Internet Applications: HTML attributes that provide extra semantics for assistive technologies when native HTML isn't sufficient

title: What is the tabindex attribute?
options: Controls the order in which elements receive focus when the user presses Tab; 0 = natural order, -1 = focusable by script only, positive = explicit order
correct_answer: Controls the order in which elements receive focus when the user presses Tab; 0 = natural order, -1 = focusable by script only, positive = explicit order

title: What is the color-scheme property?
description: What does the color-scheme CSS property do?
options: Indicates which color schemes (light/dark) the element supports, allowing the browser to adjust default UI elements accordingly
correct_answer: Indicates which color schemes (light/dark) the element supports, allowing the browser to adjust default UI elements accordingly

title: What is the accent-color property?
options: Sets the accent color for form controls like checkboxes, radio buttons, range sliders, and progress bars
correct_answer: Sets the accent color for form controls like checkboxes, radio buttons, range sliders, and progress bars

title: Build a responsive card layout.
description: Create a responsive card grid layout using CSS Grid that shows 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, 3 on desktop.

title: Build a sticky header with blur.
description: Create a sticky navigation header with a glassmorphism blur effect.

title: Build a CSS-only dark mode toggle.
description: Implement a dark mode using CSS custom properties and prefers-color-scheme.

title: Create an animated loading spinner.
description: Build a pure CSS loading spinner animation.

title: Build a tooltip with CSS only.
description: Create a tooltip that appears on hover using only CSS.

title: What is the scroll-snap property?
options: Provides snap points for scrolling containers so the viewport locks to specific child elements after scrolling
correct_answer: Provides snap points for scrolling containers so the viewport locks to specific child elements after scrolling

title: What is the overflow property?
options: visible (default, content overflows), hidden (content clipped), scroll (always shows scrollbar), auto (scrollbar when needed)
correct_answer: visible (default, content overflows), hidden (content clipped), scroll (always shows scrollbar), auto (scrollbar when needed)

title: What is the calc() function?
options: Performs math calculations with mixed units in CSS property values, e.g., calc(100% - 2rem)
correct_answer: Performs math calculations with mixed units in CSS property values, e.g., calc(100% - 2rem)

title: What is the min() and max() function?
description: What do min() and max() do in CSS?
options: min() returns the smallest value; max() returns the largest; useful for responsive sizing, e.g., width: min(100%, 600px)
correct_answer: min() returns the smallest value; max() returns the largest; useful for responsive sizing, e.g., width: min(100%, 600px)

title: What is the text-overflow property?
options: Displays an ellipsis (...) when text overflows its container (requires overflow: hidden and white-space: nowrap)
correct_answer: Displays an ellipsis (...) when text overflows its container (requires overflow: hidden and white-space: nowrap)

title: What is the line-clamp technique?
description: How do you truncate text to n lines with ellipsis?
options: display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: n; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; overflow: hidden; — limits text to n lines with ellipsis
correct_answer: display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: n; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; overflow: hidden; — limits text to n lines with ellipsis

title: What is the content-visibility property?
options: Skips rendering of off-screen elements until they're near the viewport, dramatically improving initial render performance for long pages
correct_answer: Skips rendering of off-screen elements until they're near the viewport, dramatically improving initial render performance for long pages

title: What is the :focus-visible pseudo-class?
options: :focus-visible only matches when the browser determines focus should be visually indicated (keyboard navigation), avoiding focus rings on mouse clicks
correct_answer: :focus-visible only matches when the browser determines focus should be visually indicated (keyboard navigation), avoiding focus rings on mouse clicks

title: What is the place-items shorthand?
description: What does place-items do in Grid?
options: Shorthand for align-items and justify-items; place-items: center centers all grid items both horizontally and vertically
correct_answer: Shorthand for align-items and justify-items; place-items: center centers all grid items both horizontally and vertically

title: What is the isolation property?
options: Creates a new stacking context for the element, preventing its children's blend modes from affecting elements outside
correct_answer: Creates a new stacking context for the element, preventing its children's blend modes from affecting elements outside

title: What is the mix-blend-mode property?
options: Specifies how an element's content blends with the content below it (multiply, screen, overlay, etc.)
correct_answer: Specifies how an element's content blends with the content below it (multiply, screen, overlay, etc.)

title: What is the inset property?
options: Shorthand for top, right, bottom, and left simultaneously; inset: 0 sets all four to 0
correct_answer: Shorthand for top, right, bottom, and left simultaneously; inset: 0 sets all four to 0

title: What is the logical properties in CSS?
description: What are logical properties like margin-inline and padding-block?
options: Direction-agnostic properties that work with writing mode: inline for the text direction axis, block for the perpendicular axis; essential for internationalization
correct_answer: Direction-agnostic properties that work with writing mode: inline for the text direction axis, block for the perpendicular axis; essential for internationalization

title: What is the subgrid value?
options: Allows a nested grid item to adopt the track sizing of its parent grid, maintaining alignment across nested layouts
correct_answer: Allows a nested grid item to adopt the track sizing of its parent grid, maintaining alignment across nested layouts

title: Build a truncated text with 'Read More'.
description: Create CSS for a text block that shows 3 lines with ellipsis and a 'Read More' toggle.

title: Build a fluid typography system.
description: Create a fluid typography scale that smoothly transitions between viewport sizes using clamp().

title: Build a CSS-only hamburger menu animation.
description: Create a hamburger icon that animates into an X on click using CSS transitions.
